name: Yacimiento de Cerrillo Blanco y Castillo de Lopera - circular through the olive groves of Porcuna
name_original: Yacimiento de Cerrillo Blanco y Castillo de Lopera - circular por los olivares de Porcuna
region_intro: Touring cycling around Lopera, located in Spain's Jaén province, features a landscape of gentle, undulating hills dominated by extensive olive groves. The terrain also includes smaller sections of vineyards and cereal crops, with irrigated cultivation in the plains of the Salado creek. This region offers varied gradients suitable for different skill levels, providing a blend of scenic beauty and historical interest. The area's rich history is evident through landmarks such as the Castle of the Military Order of Calatrava and numerous archaeological sites. Best touring cycling routes around Lopera The most popular touring cycling route is Yacimiento de Cerrillo Blanco y Castillo de Lopera - circular through the olive groves of Porcuna, a 20.7 miles (33.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of historical sites and extensive olive groves. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Castillo de Lopera – Cañete de las Torres loop from Lopera, a difficult 29.6 miles (47.6 km) path. This route provides a challenging ride through the Lopera countryside, connecting local towns and landmarks. Local touring cyclists also love the Puente de los Tres Ojos loop from Casas Nuevas, a 24.3 miles (39.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. Touring cycling around Lopera is defined by undulating hills, vast olive groves, and historical sites like the Castle of Calatrava. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides through agricultural landscapes to more difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Lopera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lopera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Lopera?
A: The terrain around Lopera is characterized by gentle, undulating hills, a classic feature of the Jaén countryside. You'll primarily cycle through vast olive groves, which provide a picturesque backdrop. There are also smaller sections with vineyards and cereal crops, and irrigated cultivation in the plains of the Salado creek, adding to the diverse agricultural scenery.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Lopera area?
A: There are nearly 60 touring cycling routes around Lopera listed on komoot. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from easy rides through agricultural landscapes to more challenging paths with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Lopera offers a few easy touring cycling routes. While most routes are moderate or difficult, there are 2 routes classified as easy, perfect for those looking for a more relaxed ride through the scenic olive groves and countryside.

Q: What historical sites or natural features can I see while cycling around Lopera?
A: Lopera is rich in history and natural beauty. You can encounter landmarks such as the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996425484/yacimiento-de-cerrillo-blanco-y-castillo-de-lopera-circular-through-the-olive-groves-of-porcuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Military Order of Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable attractions in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmolejo Spa&lt;/a&gt;, the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montoro&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6443221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Montoro&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features numerous archaeological sites and charming chapels.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Lopera?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Lopera are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996425484/yacimiento-de-cerrillo-blanco-y-castillo-de-lopera-circular-through-the-olive-groves-of-porcuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento de Cerrillo Blanco y Castillo de Lopera - circular through the olive groves of Porcuna&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Lopera?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Lopera is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable exploration of the undulating hills and olive groves.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Lopera?
A: The touring cycling routes in Lopera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the picturesque landscapes dominated by vast olive groves, the varied gradients suitable for different skill levels, and the blend of scenic beauty with historical interest provided by landmarks like the Castle of Calatrava.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Lopera offers 29 difficult touring cycling routes for those seeking a challenge. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40311735" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Lopera – Cañete de las Torres loop from Lopera&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a demanding ride through the countryside, connecting local towns and landmarks.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through different types of agricultural landscapes?
A: Yes, the Lopera region showcases diverse agricultural landscapes. Beyond the extensive olive groves, you can find routes that pass through areas with vineyards, cereal crops, and even irrigated cultivation of crops like cotton, sunflower, sugar beet, and maize in the plains of the Salado creek. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21846444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Tres Ojos loop from Casas Nuevas&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes starting near Lopera?
A: For those looking for longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31512904" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmolejo Spa – San Ginés Hermitage loop from Villa del Rio&lt;/a&gt; offer a significant challenge, covering nearly 100 km with substantial elevation gain, providing an extensive exploration of the wider region.

Q: What makes Lopera a unique destination for touring cyclists?
A: Lopera's unique appeal for touring cyclists lies in its distinctive combination of gently undulating hills covered in vast olive groves, its rich historical heritage including the Castle of the Military Order of Calatrava and numerous archaeological sites, and its diverse agricultural scenery. This blend offers both physical activity and cultural immersion, set against a backdrop that was even chosen as a stage finish for the professional Vuelta a Andalucía race.
tips: Extraordinary piece to understand not only the importance of the "oppidum" túrdulo de Porcuna, but also the history of Alta Andalucía itself. The set of sculptures located in this settlement is, by far, the most important and monumental that Turdetan and Túrdula archeology has provided, and the key to understanding this culture at the time of its full consolidation, during the 5th century BC.
A well preserved castle and a professional guide. I recommend it without a doubt. Prior appointment is required to make the visit.

waypoints.name: Mezza Pleca Tomb, Cerrillo Blanco Archaeological Site
